entry: CSS color-mix() lets you blend two colors in a given color space — great for generating transparent tints from a single token without maintaining a separate alpha variable.
IntersectionObserver with a negative rootMargin is the cleanest way to track which section is active in a single-page site without scroll event listeners.
A CNAME file in the repo root is all GitHub Pages needs to serve a custom domain — no DNS-provider-side TTL tricks required if the A records are already set.
Atom One Dark's palette works well as a design system because every color already has a clear semantic role — bg, fg, muted, and six accent hues with built-in contrast.
scroll-margin-top on sections offsets anchor-link jumps by exactly the sticky nav height, so the heading doesn't hide behind the bar after clicking a nav link.
